title: Paragraph Objects
intro: `ip` selects the paragraph under the cursor and `ap` includes the blank line after it. Combine them with operators — `dip`, `dap`, `yip`, `vip` — to move whole blocks at once.

exercises.instruction: Delete the first block with `dip` — the blank line stays.
exercises.initialCode:
old line one
old line two

keep this line
hints: `ip` covers every line of the paragraph, but not the blank line after it.

exercises.instruction: Delete the first block AND the gap with `dap` — the next block slides up.
Duplicate the block: `yip` copies it, `p` pastes it below the current line.

goal.buffer:
setup();

step one
step two
step one
step two

hints: `yip` yanks linewise, so `p` pastes whole lines below the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Select the function block with `vip`, then indent the whole selection with `>`.
hints: Visual mode + text object: `vip` grabs the block, `>` shifts it.

exercises.instruction: Remove the middle block and its trailing gap with `dap`.
exercises.initialCode:
keep top

middle block a
middle block b

keep bottom

exercises.instruction: Delete the scratch block at the end with `dap` — with no blank line after it, `ap` takes the one before.
hints: At the end of the file, `ap` reaches backward for the blank line.
